economic_notes: GBEs in energy and mining sectors: Twin Falls LP (hydro electric, 97% owned), Umbata Falls LP (hydro, 51% owned), Biiwaabikoke LP (mining labour services, 100% owned), Kagiano Power Corporation (100% owned). Government Business Partnership: BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP (48% owned, forestry/mining/energy/construction). Capacity funding from Williams Operating Corporation. Road maintenance services contract with Nawiinginokiima Forest Management Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022. Consolidated entities include Biigtigong Dbenjgan Ltd., Begetekong Trust, and MKWA Contracting GP Ltd. Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Twin Falls LP, Umbata Falls LP, Biiwaabikoke LP, and Kagiano Power Corporation. One Government Business Partnership: BMI-Maamwigaaboo Contracting LP. Large deferred revenue balance of $17.1M primarily due to ISC Education Centre funding ($12.8M deferred). Twin Falls LP recorded a combined 2020 and 2021 loss of $2,288,684 due to prior year financial statements not being available. Contingent asset: outstanding Aboriginal Rights and Title claim against Crown. RBC demand loan of $6.6M due February 2023 or earlier settlement of land claims. OSR calculated as total revenue $23,355,812 minus total government transfers (federal $8,502,543 + provincial $4,086,484) = $10,766,785. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text.
